summary:
The presence of PAS-positive intracytoplasmic granules in hepatocytes strongly suggests alpha-1 antitripsyin deficiency due to its well-documented association with this specific histochemical pattern. This condition presents clinically with jaundice and cholestatic features early in infancy consistent with the patient presentation.

Final Answer: C
